title: US and Iranian Delegations Arrive for Oman Talks
detail: US special envoy Steve Witkoff and Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner arrived in Doha ahead of the Muscat talks, while Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi traveled to Oman. CENTCOM commander Admiral Brad Cooper also joined the US delegation, an unusual move that signaled the military dimension of the negotiations. The talks were brokered by Omani Foreign Minister Badr bin Hamad Al Busaidi.

title: USS Lincoln CSG Maintains Arabian Sea Patrol
detail: The USS Abraham Lincoln carrier strike group continued 24/7 flight operations in the Arabian Sea south of Iran, with F/A-18E Super Hornets and F-35C Lightning IIs flying combat air patrols. The carrier maintained a heightened defensive posture following the February 3 shootdown of an Iranian Shahed-139 drone that had approached the battle group. CENTCOM described the patrol tempo as 'sustained and deliberate.'

title: Iranian Rial Hits New Low Amid Sanctions Pressure
detail: The Iranian rial continued its slide against the US dollar, with the free-market rate approaching 150,000 tomans per dollar amid tightened sanctions enforcement. Year-on-year inflation reached 60% in January, with food prices up 72% and health costs up 50%. The economic pressure fueled ongoing protests that had begun in late December 2025, adding domestic urgency to the upcoming Muscat negotiations.
